claim_boundary.production_candidate_layer: AANA is production-candidate as an audit/control/verification/correction layer.
claim_boundary.not_proven_engine: AANA is not yet proven as a raw agent-performance engine.

implementation_tasks.task: Define the exact production-candidate boundary language.
Publish a report that states AANA is production-candidate as a control layer and not yet proven as a raw agent-performance engine.
Include failures, false positives, latency, and unsupported domains.
Link existing governance, dataset, adapter, and reporting artifacts.
Add a validator, CLI, dev command, package script, and tests for the evidence pack.

failures: Tau2-style raw task completion remains mixed when AANA is paired with a weak base scaffold.
Some long all-domain benchmark runs are incomplete or diagnostic because local run timeouts were reached.
Fixture-level held-out validation is not the same as large human-reviewed external validation.
false_positives: Safe public reads may be delayed if runtime metadata is incomplete.
Safe grounded answers may be deferred when evidence exists but is not referenced.
Conservative thresholds can reduce safe allow rate while preserving unsafe recall.
latency: No production p95 or p99 latency claim is made by this evidence pack.
Runtime latency depends on schema validation, retrieval, verifier depth, model-judged classifiers, and human-review paths.
unsupported_domains: raw autonomous task performance as a standalone engine
financial advice, trading, lending, underwriting, or irreversible financial transactions
unregistered tool families without AANA-compatible metadata
domains without held-out validation and evidence connectors
broad multilingual or multimodal claims without domain-specific validation
